title: tabebuia umbellata var. lanceolata
intro: Tabebuia umbellata var. lanceolata, also known as the Pink Trumpet Tree or Lançones, is a flowering plant native to Brazil, belonging to the Bignoniaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as lapachol. The health benefits of Tabebuia umbellata var. lanceolata include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and extracts, which are said to offer relief from various ailments and promote overall well-being.
